author: Alex Zislin
executive_summary: Alex Zislin, an active small retail investor, opposes the proposed changes to the Trade-Through Rule and Locked and Crossed Markets Provisions of Regulation NMS (File No. S7-2026-20), citing the critical importance of best price execution to his personal portfolio performance.

positions.best_execution: Strongly supportive of maintaining best execution protections; opposes changes that may weaken them

economic_arguments: Best price execution is essential to maximizing retail investor portfolio returns
Changes to the Trade-Through Rule could negatively impact the prices retail investors receive on their orders
retail_investor_arguments: As an active small investor, the author is directly and personally affected by order execution quality
Weakening the Trade-Through Rule protections could harm retail investors who rely on best price guarantees
Retail investors depend on Regulation NMS protections to ensure they receive the best available prices in the market
recommended_actions: Reject or withdraw the proposed changes to the Trade-Through Rule and Locked and Crossed Markets Provisions of Regulation NMS
key_quotes: I wish to register opposition to the proposed changes to S7-2026-20 == The Trade-Through Rule and Locked and Crossed Markets Provisions of Regulation NMS.
As an active small investor, best price execution of orders is extremely important to maximizing my portfolio.
